reason_codes: main_gru_svm.py: chosen as the sequence-model hybrid entrypoint (GRU+SVM) to satisfy sequential-model and hybrid-model claims (claim_03, claim_07).
main_mlp.py: chosen as a dense neural-network baseline to satisfy feedforward baseline and scalability claims (claim_01, claim_04, claim_10).

main_logistic_regression.py: chosen as a classification baseline and designated metric observer (emits classification metrics such as accuracy/loss); supports evaluation and monitoring claims (claim_02, claim_08).
main_linear_regression.py: chosen as a regression baseline and numeric-metrics observer (emits MSE/RMSE); supports regression and numeric-evaluation claims (claim_05, claim_09).
main_nearest_neighbor.py: chosen as the non-parametric baseline to cover instance-based methods and robustness claims (claim_06, claim_12, claim_14).
metric_observers: at least one metric observer included (main_logistic_regression.py and main_linear_regression.py provide observable metrics).

coverage_note: remaining claims (claim_11, claim_13, claim_15) are not tied to a unique single-entrypoint but are expected to be addressed via combined evaluation and existing scripts' instrumentation.
